results.possibleIdentity: Ed Conroy was an engineer at Grumman Aircraft (later Northrop Grumman) active during the late 1960s and 1970s. According to technical records, he was based in Selden, New York, and specialized in the technical fields of data processing and instrumentation. He was a participant in Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded to foster collaborations between artists and engineers. Conroy's professional background at Grumman align with the company's significant roles in aerospace projects of the era, such as the Lunar Module and advanced aircraft systems, which required extensive expertise in instrumentation and data management. In the 1990s, an Edward Conroy from Northrop Grumman was recognized as a contributor to the EIA-731.1 Systems Engineering Capability Model, suggesting a long-term career in systems engineering and technical standards within the aerospace industry.
